# Transcrição: Top_5_Reasons_to_Become_a_BETTER_Listener

## [00:00 - 02:50]

Hello. In this video I want to talk to you about why listening is so important when it comes to communication. This question was sent in by Barbara, so Barbara, thank you for asking this question. You know, being a professional speaker, I'm actually a lot better at speaking than listening, so this is going to be almost a difficult question for me to answer, but it's definitely a goal of mine in my personal life and my professional life to be a better listener. Here are the top five things that I keep in mind when I try to motivate myself to be a better listener. The first thing is, when you're speaking, you're just repeating what you know. When you're listening, that's when you're actually learning something new. The second thing, every single human being on this earth actually has a gold nugget that you can learn from, that if you actually dig for the gold nugget, it can change your life. The third reason, a big part of why we don't want to listen to other people is often we're scared that other people might have an opposing view to the one we have, but imagine you lived in a world where everybody believes the same thing you believe and they believe you, you believe them, then you're essentially living in an echo chamber. Who wants to live in that kind of a world? This is why we need to listen, because the perspectives of other people actually matter, because they can see things you can't see. The fourth thing, here's a really good one, might I say so for myself, most people in the world, they aren't listening, they're just waiting for their turn to speak. So imagine that. Imagine we live in a world where the majority of people are waiting for their turn to speak and no one's actually listening, guess what? Your unique value proposition, you being able to be different, is you actually listening and then all of a sudden you're different to the 99% of others out there. And the fifth one and final one, but it's not the last thing because I'm sure you would have many other things you could write down in the comment section below as to why you think listening is important. But for me, the fifth thing is when you listen, you're able to build more meaningful connections with other people. These are the top five things that I keep at the front of my mind to really keep me motivated to be a better listener. But you see, these are just reasons why you should listen. How do you listen better if we think about it from a pragmatic sense? How do you pragmatically listen better or be a better listener? Simple. Ask more questions. Instead of saying what you think about a specific topic when somebody's talking to you, be more curious about what they're talking about and ask more questions. And as a result of asking more questions, you'll have more to listen to. Let me give you an example. So if somebody was telling me about their wonderful weekend they had away with their family and they went jet skiing, I'd instead of saying, oh, I've been on a jet ski too and I can jet ski as well, I'd say, tell me, where did you jet ski? What lake was it? I'd ask them more questions about what they're saying to better dig more out of them so I have more to listen to. I guess this is why Dale Carnegie said that you can make more friends in two months by being interested in other people than you can in two years by trying to get other people interested in you. Being a great listener really pays off.
